summary: Exit-family archetype with a single supported entry-filter dimension; structurally distinct ladder shape (60/40 vs prior 50/30/20) and likely tight-but-usable sample size, though prior multi_tp baseline performance is a material headwind worth flagging.

analyses.distinctness_notes: Novel ladder shape (60/40 two-step) is structurally different from prior 50/30/20 three-step multi_tp ladders. Front-loaded 60% harvest at TP1=0.5% changes the realized-PnL distribution shape (less reliance on TP3 hit rate, lower variance per trade). Filter dimension taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 narrows to high-conviction taker-flow regimes, which differs from generic multi_tp baselines that operate on full population.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Exit-side archetype consumes any compatible entry triggers. Across all channels ~7200 events/30d; taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 cut rate ≈p35-p40 of distribution → ~0.4 retention → ~2880 base events. However exit-archetypes in this loop typically attach to filtered entry sets, so realized fills ≈300-500/30d for the matched entry-channel slice (conservatively 300).
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: This IS an exit-family proposal (multi_tp). Compatibility is with itself by definition. Material concern: by_exit_family stats show multi_tp aggregate mean=-0.276, median=-0.28, n_positive=0/3111 in current registry — the family has shown zero positive instances. The 60/40 front-loaded shape is the right hypothesis to test (reduces dependence on long tail), but Synthesizer should weigh that this is fighting a strong negative prior. Recommend pairing with high-lift long entries on pricechange/long (E126/E064/E148 family) where TP1=0.5% is reachable given observed +0.4 score regimes. Single-condition filter (taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60) is computable and parseable; no fallbacks needed.
